subtask_3.prose: scripts/pick-model.sh:67-72 sources a per-user override from either DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG or $HOME/.claude/skills/delegate-local/config.sh, executing arbitrary bash inheriting the caller's environment. The behaviour is intentional for single-user development but widens the trust surface in sudo, shared-HOME CI, or service-account contexts.

scripts/pick-model.sh:67 gives the DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG environment variable higher precedence than the $HOME-relative default, so any process able to pre-set the variable can redirect override sourcing without needing filesystem write access. This is the same threat model as F1 but easier to exploit when the calling process's environment is not fully controlled.

scripts/init.sh:62-69 wraps each model name in double quotes via printf with no escape pass. A model name containing a literal double-quote or dollar sign would produce broken or expansion-bearing config.sh content when sourced.

scripts/init.sh:33-43 parses pick-model.sh's case block via perl regex with no integrity check on the script's content. If pick-model.sh is compromised upstream, init.sh propagates the malicious patterns into per-user override configs.

scripts/pick-model.sh:69-72 sources the override under set -euo pipefail, so a malformed override produces an unhelpful exit code from pick-model.sh. This is informational rather than exploitable but degrades the personalisation feature's UX.

subtask_4:
Reviewing Phase 9 v1 of delegate-local — adds per-user override hook to scripts/pick-model.sh and a new scripts/init.sh. 5 findings: 2 medium, 2 low, 1 info.

- [MEDIUM] F1 — Override path sources arbitrary bash via env var or $HOME
- [MEDIUM] F2 — DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG env var has higher precedence than $HOME path
- [LOW] F3 — init.sh emits unescaped model names from ollama list into generated bash
- [LOW] F4 — init.sh parses pick-model.sh's case block via perl regex without integrity check
- [INFO] F5 — Override sourcing fails noisily under set -e propagation

All findings are documented in the longer review. The maintainer should weigh REAL findings against the documented design intent (single-user developer context, parity with aider/Claude-Code override conventions).
